instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
What is Army Postal Life Insurance?
One of the earliest life insurance schemes introduced in our country was Postal life insurance. These insurance schemes were low-premium and highly convenient financial products to safeguard the financial future of family members for every breadwinner. Understanding the ultimate necessity of life protection coverage for the people working in the defence sector, the Army Postal Service (APS) took over the responsibility of Postal Life Insurance (PLI) to provide their families with sufficient financial support during unforeseen events.
What is Postal Life Insurance for Army?
Army postal life insurance is a specially designed life insurance plan for armed forces personnel to secure the financial well-being of their family even when they are not around in this world. Basically, postal life insurance for the army is an army group insurance that provides necessary life protection to all the personnel of the armed forces at a lower rate of premium. Let’s take a look at the eligibility criteria for army postal life insurance.
Eligibility Criteria for Army Postal Life Insurance
Defence personnel belonging to all ranks are eligible for postal life insurance coverage offered by the Army Postal Service (APS). Here is the list of some of the armed forces personnel who avail the benefit of army group insurance:
Border Roads Organization personnel
Short-service commissioned officers
Border Security Force
Central Industrial Security Force
Indo-Tibetan Border Police (ITBP)
Assam Rifles
Non-combatants of the Army, Air Force, and Navy
Eligibility criteria for entry age, sum assured, and the other benefits of the policy may vary depending on the type of army postal insurance provided to the armed force personnel.
Limits in Postal Life Insurance for Army Personnel
Coverage limits in Army postal life insurance vary depending on the type of postal life insurance. The limit varies from INR 10,000 to INR 50,00,000.
Features of Army Postal Life Insurance
The following are the features of Army postal life insurance:
Loan against the policy: Army postal life insurance can be used as collateral for availing of the loan. However, the loan can be availed after 3 years and 4 years for the endowment policy and whole life insurance policy, respectively.
Policy renewal: A lapsed policy can be renewed based on the policy conditions:
If the policy was active for less than three years and lapsed for non-payment of 6 consecutive premium amounts.
If the policy was active for more than three years and lapsed for non-payment of 12 consecutive premium amounts.
Nomination facility: The policy gives you the flexibility to change the nomination during the policy period.
Policy conversion: There are different types of Army postal life insurance plans available, and the policyholder has the option to convert from one type to another, such as from a whole life policy to an endowment plan.
Duplicate policy: In case the policyholder misplaces or loses the original policy document, the duplicate policy can be availed.
Benefits of Army Postal Life Insurance
The following are the benefits of Army postal life insurance:
Cost-effective life insurance coverage for the armed force personnel
Flexible premium payment option – yearly, half-yearly, or monthly
Nomination facility along with the change of nomination option
Quick and hassle-free claim settlement
No medicals
Tax benefit under Section 88 of the Income Tax Act, 19611
Types of Army Postal Life Insurance Schemes2
The following are the postal life insurance schemes provided for armed force personnel:
Suraksha Whole Life Insurance
Sum assured with the accumulated bonus is paid out to the policyholder at the age of 80 years or to the nominee if the policyholder dies during the policy term.
Age criteria – 19 years to 55 years
The sum assured can vary from INR 20,000 to INR 50,00,000
Loan facility after 3 years
Policy surrender is allowed after 3 years
The policy can be converted to an endowment plan after a year and can be converted till the policyholder reaches 57 years of age.
Suvidha Convertible Whole Life Assurance Plan
The plan can be converted to an endowment assurance plan after the completion of 5 years.
Age criteria – 19 years to 50 years
The sum assured can vary from INR 20,000 to INR 50,00,000
Loan facility after four years
Policy surrender is allowed after 3 years.
Santhosh Endowment Assurance Plan
Sum assured with the accumulated bonus will be paid on maturity to the policyholder or to the nominee in case of the policyholder's untimely demise during the policy tenure.
Age criteria – 19 years to 50 years
The sum assured can vary from INR 20,000 to INR 50,00,000
Loan facility after 3 years
Policy surrender is allowed after 3 years
Sumangal Anticipated Endowment Assurance Plan
This plan is a money-back policy that makes periodic payouts to the policyholder. In case of policyholder dies during the policy term, the sum assured in a lump sum is paid out to the beneficiary.
Age criteria – 19 years to 40 years (for a policy term of 20 years) and 19 years to 45 years (for a policy term of 15 years)
Sum assured can go up to INR 50,00,000
Money back survival benefit for –
A policy with 15 years tenure – 20% of the sum assured each at the end of 6, 9, and 12 years respectively. 40%+bonus at the end of maturity
A policy with 20 years tenure - 20% of the sum assured each at the end of 8, 12, and 16 years respectively. 40%+bonus at the end of maturity
Yugal Suraksha Joint Life Assurance Plan
This plan allows the inclusion of spouses under Army postal life insurance to provide a joint life cover.
Age criteria – 21 years to 45 years.
Policy tenure options vary from 5 years to 20 years.
Loan facility after 3 years.
Policy surrender is allowed after 3 years.
Bal Jeevan Bhima Children Policy
Armed force personnel can avail of life insurance coverage for their children through this plan. The sum assured can vary from INR 20,000 to INR 1,00,000.
Maturity benefit is the sum assured + accumulated bonus or the sum assured as a death benefit to the beneficiary.
Army Postal Life Insurance Status
You can check the status of your postal life insurance policy through your online account or by sending an email to pli.dte@gmail.com. Alternatively, you can call on postal life insurance customer care number 18001156993.
Postal life insurance is cost-effective, high coverage, and convenient option for armed personnel to financially safeguard their family.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Ltd., Regd. office Address: Bajaj Allianz House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006, Reg. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, Call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in, Fax No: 02066026789

Postal life Insurance (indiapost.gov.in)
APS Home (indianarmy.nic.in)
